titleStmt.title: SII 28.9: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 9. (A.R. No. 9 of 1913.) PERAMBALŪR, PERAMBALUR TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: The inscription is prefaced with the statement aruḷi-cheyal indicating that what follows is a royal order. But the name of the king is not given.

Addressed to the nāṭṭavar of the mēl-kūṟṟu of Vembār-nāḍu, the record says that (the village) Nochchiyam had in the past been granted as iṟaiili (tax-free) designated as nandavana-ppuṟa-iṟai-ili (tax-free land meant to be enjoyed as maintenance by those tending the flower-garden attached to the temple) and was being utilised for (meeting the expenses of) worship, maintenance of temple premises and for procession during festivals of God Gōpāla[r]-kūttar-viṇṇakar-emberumān, but later came to be taxed (tiṟappāy) and remained so till the tenth year. Now this order restored the village to the iṟai-ili status for the same purposes as originally intended. However, it was specified that the (annual) festival should be so arranged so that the tittam (tīrtham) (the valedictory function) should fall on the day of chōti (svāti) in the month of Chittirai, the birth-day (of the king) who issued this order. It was further specified that the iṟai-ili lands of bhaṭṭar (brahmins) and pulavar (poets) and the uḷḷūr-nāyanmār and what was earmarked as tiruviḷakkuppuṟam (land the proceeds from which were to maintain the lamps) were excepted from the purview of the grant. It is stated that the grant of the village of the God as iṟai-ili now meant that the levies, uḷḷūr-nāyanār-dūta-vari, śī-kāriya-ppēru, kāṇikkai, natta-vari, maravaḍai, kuḷavaḍai, taṟi-iṟai, chekk-iṟai, veṭṭi, āṇaikkūḍam, kudiraippanti and all other such āyams were exempted from payment or were entitled to be levied and enjoyed for the temple purposes. The order was attested by Sundara-pāṇḍiya-Viḻupparaiyaṉ and Vaḍa-gōpuram-uḍaiyān Kaḍalaḍaitta-viḻupparaiyaṉ of Iḷaṅguḍi alias Indiravatāranallūr in Vaḍa-vallattirukkai in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . Aruḷiceyal . vempār nāṭṭu me
l kūṟṟu nāṭṭavaṟku perumpālūril kopāla
r kūttar viṇṇakar Emperumāṉukku tirunan
tavaṉappuṟa Iṟaiyiliyāy tiruvārātaṉamum tiru
ppaṇiyum naṭatti tirunāḷum Eḻuntaruḷivippatāka
muṟkālattil kalveṭṭiṉapaṭi Iṟaiyiliyāy pi
ṉpu tiṟappāy pattāvatu varaiyum vāram koṇṭu
ponta nocciyam piṉpilāṇṭil kalveṭ
ṭiṉpaṭiye Iṉṉāyaṉāṟku tiruvārātāṉamum tiruppa
ṇiyum naṭatti cittirai mātam nām piṟanta nāḷ coti
yile nittam prasādittaruḷūvatāka tirunāḷ Eḻuntaru
ḷūvatākavum Ivvūr nāṉkellaikkuḷpaṭṭa ṉance
y puṉceyil paṭṭarkaḷ pulavarkaḷ Iṟaiyiliyum Uḷ
ḷūr nāyamār tiruviḷakkuppuṟa Iṟaiyiliyum Uḷḷamai
yum nikki Uḷḷūr ṉāyamār tūtavari cikāriyappeṟum
kāṇikkaiyum nattavariyum maravaṭai kuḷavaṭaiyum
taṟiyiṟai maṉaiyiṟai cekkiṟai veṭṭi Āṉaik kū
ṭam kutiraipanti maṟṟum Eṟpeṟ paṭṭa Aṉaittā
yaṅkaḷum Uḷpaṭa Iṉṉāyaṉāṟkku Iṟaiyiliyāka
kuṭuttom Ippaṭikku Ivvolai piṭipāṭākak
koṇṭu kallilum cempilum veṭṭivittu Iv
vūr nāṉkellaiyilum tiruvāḻik kallum nāṭṭi
on a seperate piece probably in continuation of the above.

kkāṭṭiṉom Ivai cuntarapāṇṭiya viḻupparaiyaṉ Eḻuttu pāṇṭimaṇṭalattu vaṭavallat tirukkai Iḷāṉkuṭi Āṉa Intiraavatāra nallūr vaṭakopura muṭaiyāṉ kaṭal Aṭaitta perumāḷāṉa cuntara pāṇṭiya viḻupparaiyaṉon a seperate piece— kuṭukka ippaṭi colluvatāka tūtaṉai pa
one piece missing.ttu vikitamApparently it stands for likhitam. §
Digital edition of SII 28.9 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
